---
title: "Blue Space for Nature"
author: "Matthew Morgan"
date: "2025-05-16"
output: html_document
---

```{r load packages}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(ggplot2)
library(MASS)
library(broom)
library(car)
library(pscl)
library(tibble)
library(car)
library(lmtest)
library(knitr)
library(broom)
library(patchwork)
library(DHARMa)
library(forcats)
library(SuppDists)
library(brant)  
library(ordinal)
library(corrplot)
library(gghalves)
```

```{r load data from directory}
model_data <- read_excel("model_data.xlsx")
```

```{r variable diagnostics}
# dependent variables
table(model_data$connection_group)
table(model_data$provision)
table(model_data$`6. Please click the item that best describes the neighbourhood you live in:`)
hist(model_data$blue_space_attitude) # left skew

# independent variables numeric
hist(model_data$`2. What is your current age in years?`) # normal
hist(model_data$total_activities) # right skew
hist(model_data$blue_space_total_frequency) # right skew
hist(model_data$blue_space_mean_frequency) # right skew
hist(model_data$`12.3. Blue spaces in the UK are in poor condition`)
table(model_data$`6. Please click the item that best describes the neighbourhood you live in:`)
table(model_data$`3. What is your gender?`)
```

```{r ordered_factors}
questions <- colnames(model_data)
questions

hist(model_data$composite_score)

model_data <- model_data %>%
  rename(
    heard_rewilding = `11. Have you heard of the term "rewilding"?`, #yes/no
    blue_space_condition = `12.3. Blue spaces in the UK are in poor condition`, # likert 1-7
    neighbourhood = `6. Please click the item that best describes the neighbourhood you live in:`, # categorical
    age = `2. What is your current age in years?` # continuous
  )

# varaiable structure
str(model_data$heard_rewilding)
str(model_data$blue_space_condition)
str(model_data$neighbourhood)
str(model_data$gender)
str(model_data$age)
str(model_data$age_factor)
str(model_data$total_activities)
str(model_data$provision)
str(model_data$blue_space_attitude)

# Convert to factors
model_data$heard_rewilding <- factor(model_data$heard_rewilding)
model_data$neighbourhood <- factor(model_data$neighbourhood)
model_data$gender <- factor(model_data$gender)
model_data$age_factor <- ordered(model_data$age_factor, levels = c("1", "2", "3", "4"))
model_data$provision <- factor(model_data$provision, levels = c("Maybe_No", "Provisional_Yes", "Yes"), ordered = TRUE)
model_data$connection_group <- factor(model_data$connection_group, levels = c("Low", "Medium", "High"),ordered = TRUE)

# correlation matrix
# Specify the numeric variables of interest

vars_to_correlate <- c(
  "blue_space_attitude",
  "blue_space_mean_frequency",
  "blue_space_num_visited",
  "total_activities",
  "age",
  "blue_space_condition"
)

# Subset the data to just those variables
subset_data <- model_data[, vars_to_correlate]

# Step 1: Convert ordered factor to numeric
subset_data <- subset_data %>%
  mutate(blue_space_condition_num = as.numeric(blue_space_condition))

subset_data_for_cor <- subset_data %>%
  dplyr::select(-blue_space_condition) %>%
  dplyr::rename(blue_space_condition = blue_space_condition_num)


# Compute Spearman correlation (better for ordinal data)
cor_matrix <- cor(subset_data_for_cor, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = "spearman")

print(cor_matrix)

# Optional: visualize with corrplot if installed
if (!require(corrplot)) install.packages("corrplot")
library(corrplot)

corrplot(cor_matrix, method = "color", 
         type = "upper", 
         tl.col = "black", 
         tl.srt = 45,
         addCoef.col = "black", 
         number.cex = 0.6,
         diag = FALSE)

# remove outliers
# clean_data <- model_data[-c(2, 1, 33, 125), ]# optional but chose not too.
```

## Nature Connection and Engagement

```{r nature connection predictors model FULL}
#
lm_model <- lm(
  rep(1, nrow(model_data )) ~ 
    connection_group +
    blue_space_mean_frequency +
    blue_space_num_visited +
    total_activities +
    blue_space_condition +
    neighbourhood +
    gender  +
    age +
    provision +
    blue_space_attitude,
  data = model_data 
)

# 
vif(lm_model)


# regression model
sig_model1 <- polr(
  connection_group ~ 
    blue_space_mean_frequency +
    blue_space_num_visited +
    total_activities +
    blue_space_condition +
    neighbourhood +
    gender  +
    age +
    provision +
    blue_space_attitude,
  data = model_data ,
  Hess = TRUE
)

# Summary of the model
summary(sig_model1)
brant(sig_model1)

# Step 1: Coefficient table with p-values
coef_table <- coef(summary(sig_model1))
p_values <- pnorm(abs(coef_table[, "t value"]), lower.tail = FALSE) * 2

# Step 2: Remove threshold/intercept rows
coef_table_no_thresh <- coef_table[!grepl("\\|", rownames(coef_table)), ]

# Step 3: Calculate odds ratios only for predictors
odds_ratios <- exp(coef_table_no_thresh[, "Value"])

# Step 4: Combine into a clean table
coef_table_final <- cbind(
  Estimate = coef_table_no_thresh[, "Value"],
  `Std. Error` = coef_table_no_thresh[, "Std. Error"],
  `t value` = coef_table_no_thresh[, "t value"],
  `p value` = pnorm(abs(coef_table_no_thresh[, "t value"]), lower.tail = FALSE) * 2,
  `Odds Ratio` = odds_ratios
)

# Step 5: Display
kable(coef_table_final, digits = 3)


#
pR2(sig_model1)  # replace att_model with your model object name
null_model <- polr(connection_group ~ 1, data = model_data, Hess = TRUE)
anova(null_model, sig_model1)

# Residual deviance and degrees of freedom
deviance(sig_model1)
df.residual(sig_model1)
```

```{r nature connection predictors MIUNS gender}
# regression model
sig_model2 <- polr(
  connection_group ~ 
    blue_space_mean_frequency +
    blue_space_num_visited +
    total_activities +
    blue_space_condition +
    neighbourhood +
    age +
    provision +
    blue_space_attitude,
  data = model_data ,
  Hess = TRUE
)

# Summary of the model
summary(sig_model2)
brant(sig_model2)
AIC(sig_model1, sig_model2)

# Step 1: Coefficient table with p-values
coef_table <- coef(summary(sig_model2))
p_values <- pnorm(abs(coef_table[, "t value"]), lower.tail = FALSE) * 2

# Step 2: Remove threshold/intercept rows
coef_table_no_thresh <- coef_table[!grepl("\\|", rownames(coef_table)), ]

# Step 3: Calculate odds ratios only for predictors
odds_ratios <- exp(coef_table_no_thresh[, "Value"])

# Step 4: Combine into a clean table
coef_table_final <- cbind(
  Estimate = coef_table_no_thresh[, "Value"],
  `Std. Error` = coef_table_no_thresh[, "Std. Error"],
  `t value` = coef_table_no_thresh[, "t value"],
  `p value` = pnorm(abs(coef_table_no_thresh[, "t value"]), lower.tail = FALSE) * 2,
  `Odds Ratio` = odds_ratios
)

# Step 5: Display
kable(coef_table_final, digits = 3)

#
pR2(sig_model2)  # replace att_model with your model object name
null_model <- polr(connection_group ~ 1, data = model_data, Hess = TRUE)
anova(null_model, sig_model2)

# Residual deviance and degrees of freedom
deviance(sig_model2)
df.residual(sig_model2)
```

```{r nature connection predictors MIUNS gender & neighbourhood}
# regression model
sig_model3 <- polr(
  connection_group ~ 
    blue_space_mean_frequency +
    blue_space_num_visited +
    total_activities +
    blue_space_condition +
    age +
    provision +
    blue_space_attitude,
  data = model_data ,
  Hess = TRUE
)

AIC(sig_model1, sig_model2, sig_model3)

# Summary of the model
summary(sig_model3)
brant(sig_model3)

# Step 1: Coefficient table with p-values
coef_table <- coef(summary(sig_model3))
p_values <- pnorm(abs(coef_table[, "t value"]), lower.tail = FALSE) * 2

# Step 2: Remove threshold/intercept rows
coef_table_no_thresh <- coef_table[!grepl("\\|", rownames(coef_table)), ]

# Step 3: Calculate odds ratios only for predictors
odds_ratios <- exp(coef_table_no_thresh[, "Value"])

# Step 4: Combine into a clean table
coef_table_final <- cbind(
  `Estimate` = coef_table_no_thresh[, "Value"],
  `Std. Error` = coef_table_no_thresh[, "Std. Error"],
  `t value` = coef_table_no_thresh[, "t value"],
  `p value` = pnorm(abs(coef_table_no_thresh[, "t value"]), lower.tail = FALSE) * 2,
  `Odds Ratio` = odds_ratios
)

# Step 5: Display
kable(coef_table_final, digits = 3)

# Model checks
null_model <- polr(connection_group ~ 1, data = model_data, Hess = TRUE)
anova(sig_model3, null_model, test = "Chisq")
brant(sig_model3)
pR2(sig_model3)  # replace att_model with your model object name
AIC(sig_model1, sig_model2, sig_model3)

# Residual deviance and degrees of freedom
deviance(sig_model3)
df.residual(sig_model3)
```

```{r nature connection predictors MIUNS gender & neighbourhood & age}
#
model_data$blue_space_condition_num <-
  as.numeric(as.character(model_data$blue_space_condition))


# regression model
sig_model4 <- polr(
  connection_group ~ 
    blue_space_mean_frequency +
    blue_space_num_visited +
    total_activities +
    blue_space_condition +
    provision +
    blue_space_attitude,
  data = model_data ,
  Hess = TRUE
)

AIC(sig_model1, sig_model2, sig_model3, sig_model4) # model 4 is the most parsimonious 

# Summary of the model
summary(sig_model4)
brant(sig_model4)

# Step 1: Coefficient table with p-values
coef_table <- coef(summary(sig_model4))
p_values <- pnorm(abs(coef_table[, "t value"]), lower.tail = FALSE) * 2

# Step 2: Remove threshold/intercept rows
coef_table_no_thresh <- coef_table[!grepl("\\|", rownames(coef_table)), ]

# Step 3: Calculate odds ratios only for predictors
odds_ratios <- exp(coef_table_no_thresh[, "Value"])

# Step 4: Combine into a clean table
coef_table_final <- cbind(
  `Estimate` = coef_table_no_thresh[, "Value"],
  `Std. Error` = coef_table_no_thresh[, "Std. Error"],
  `t value` = coef_table_no_thresh[, "t value"],
  `p value` = pnorm(abs(coef_table_no_thresh[, "t value"]), lower.tail = FALSE) * 2,
  `Odds Ratio` = odds_ratios
)

# Step 5: Display
kable(coef_table_final, digits = 3)

# Model checks
null_model <- polr(connection_group ~ 1, data = model_data, Hess = TRUE)
anova(sig_model3, null_model, test = "Chisq")
brant(sig_model3)
pR2(sig_model3)  # replace att_model with your model object name
AIC(sig_model1, sig_model2, sig_model3)

# Residual deviance and degrees of freedom
deviance(sig_model3)
df.residual(sig_model3)
```

```{r forest plot nature connection}
# 
coef_table <- coef(summary(sig_model4))
coef_table

# Calculate p-values from t-values
p_values <- pnorm(abs(coef_table[, "t value"]), lower.tail = FALSE) * 2

#  Add p-values to the coef_table
coef_table <- cbind(coef_table, "p value" = p_values)

# Create a data frame with CIs and significance flag
coef_df <- coef_table %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  rownames_to_column(var = "Predictor") %>%
  mutate(
    lower = Value - 1.96 * `Std. Error`,
    upper = Value + 1.96 * `Std. Error`,
    signif = ifelse(`p value` < 0.05, "Significant", "Not significant")
  )

#
coef_df <- coef_df %>%
  filter(!Predictor %in% c("blue_space_condition.Q", 
"blue_space_condition.C",
"blue_space_condition^4",
"blue_space_condition^5",
"blue_space_condition^6", 
"Low|Medium", 
"Medium|High",
"provision.Q"))

rename_map <- c(
  "blue_space_condition" = "Perceived UK blue spaces to be in poor condition",
  "blue_space_mean_frequency" = "Blue space visit mean frequency",
  "blue_space_num_visited" = "Number of different blue spaces visited",
  "total_activities" = "Number of blue space activities",
  "provision.L" = "Willingness to provide a blue space",
  "blue_space_attitude" = "Positive blue space attitude"
)

#
coef_df <- coef_df %>%
  mutate(
    Predictor_clean = rename_map[Predictor],
    Predictor_clean = ifelse(is.na(Predictor_clean), Predictor, Predictor_clean)
  )

# Then convert to factor with all desired levels (including missing ones)
all_levels <- c(
  "Blue space visit mean frequency",
  "Number of different blue spaces visited",
  "Number of blue space activities",
  "Perceived UK blue spaces to be in poor condition",
  "Nature connection and engagement",
  "Willingness to provide a blue space",
  "Positive blue space attitude"
)

# Ensure Predictor_clean is a factor with the full set of levels
coef_df$Predictor_clean <- factor(coef_df$Predictor_clean, levels = c(
  "Blue space visit mean frequency",
  "Number of different blue spaces visited",
  "Number of blue space activities",
  "Perceived UK blue spaces to be in poor condition",
  "Nature connection and engagement",
  "Willingness to provide a blue space",
  "Positive blue space attitude"
))

# Check if "Nature connection and engagement" exists as data row, if not add dummy row
if (!("Nature connection and engagement" %in% coef_df$Predictor_clean)) {
  coef_df <- bind_rows(coef_df, 
    tibble(
      Predictor_clean = factor("Nature connection and engagement", levels = levels(coef_df$Predictor_clean)),
      Value = NA_real_,
      lower = NA_real_,
      upper = NA_real_,
      signif = NA_character_
    )
  )
}

p1 <- ggplot(coef_df, aes(x = factor(Predictor_clean, levels = c(
  "Blue space visit mean frequency",
  "Number of different blue spaces visited",
  "Number of blue space activities",
  "Perceived UK blue spaces to be in poor condition",
  "Nature connection and engagement",
  "Willingness to provide a blue space",
  "Positive blue space attitude"
)), y = Value, color = signif)
) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper), width = 0.2) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Nature Connection",
    x = "Predictor",
    y = "Estimate (link scale)",
    color = "Significance"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Significant" = "#0072B2", "Not significant" = "grey60"))
p1
```

## Willingness to share model

```{r willingness to share FULL model}
#
lm_model_vif1 <- lm(
  rep(1, nrow(model_data)) ~ 
    provision +
    heard_rewilding +
    gender +
    neighbourhood +
    blue_space_total_frequency +
    blue_space_num_visited +
    total_activities +
    blue_space_condition +
    connection_group +
    blue_space_attitude +
    age,
  data = model_data
)
vif(lm_model_vif1)

# Fit the logistic regression model
prov_model1 <- polr(
  provision ~ 
    blue_space_mean_frequency +
    blue_space_num_visited +
    total_activities +
    blue_space_condition +
    neighbourhood +
    gender +
    age +
    blue_space_attitude +
    connection_group,
  data = model_data,
  Hess = TRUE
)

# Model summary
summary(prov_model1)
brant(prov_model1)

# Calculate p-values
coef_table <- coef(summary(prov_model1))
p_values <- pnorm(abs(coef_table[, "t value"]), lower.tail = FALSE) * 2

# Exponentiate coefficients to get odds ratios
odds_ratios <- exp(coef(prov_model1))

# Step 1: Coefficient table with p-values
coef_table <- coef(summary(prov_model1))
p_values <- pnorm(abs(coef_table[, "t value"]), lower.tail = FALSE) * 2

# Step 2: Remove threshold/intercept rows
coef_table_no_thresh <- coef_table[!grepl("\\|", rownames(coef_table)), ]

# Step 3: Calculate odds ratios only for predictors
odds_ratios <- exp(coef_table_no_thresh[, "Value"])

# Step 4: Combine into a clean table
coef_table_final1 <- cbind(
  Estimate = coef_table_no_thresh[, "Value"],
  `Std. Error` = coef_table_no_thresh[, "Std. Error"],
  `t value` = coef_table_no_thresh[, "t value"],
  `p value` = pnorm(abs(coef_table_no_thresh[, "t value"]), lower.tail = FALSE) * 2,
  `Odds Ratio` = odds_ratios
)

# Step 5: Display
kable(coef_table_final1, digits = 3)


#
pR2(prov_model1)
null_model <- polr(provision ~ 1, data = model_data, Hess = TRUE)
anova(null_model, prov_model1)


# Residual deviance and degrees of freedom
deviance(prov_model1)
df.residual(prov_model1)
```

```{r willingness to share MINUS gender}
# Fit the logistic regression model
prov_model2 <- polr(
  provision ~ 
    blue_space_mean_frequency +
    blue_space_num_visited +
    total_activities +
    blue_space_condition +
    neighbourhood +
    age +
    blue_space_attitude +
    connection_group,
  data = model_data,
  Hess = TRUE
)

# Model summary
summary(prov_model2)
brant(prov_model2)

# Calculate p-values
coef_table <- coef(summary(prov_model2))
p_values <- pnorm(abs(coef_table[, "t value"]), lower.tail = FALSE) * 2

# Exponentiate coefficients to get odds ratios
odds_ratios <- exp(coef(prov_model2))

# Step 1: Coefficient table with p-values
coef_table <- coef(summary(prov_model1))
p_values <- pnorm(abs(coef_table[, "t value"]), lower.tail = FALSE) * 2

# Step 2: Remove threshold/intercept rows
coef_table_no_thresh <- coef_table[!grepl("\\|", rownames(coef_table)), ]

# Step 3: Calculate odds ratios only for predictors
odds_ratios <- exp(coef_table_no_thresh[, "Value"])

# Step 4: Combine into a clean table
coef_table_final1 <- cbind(
  Estimate = coef_table_no_thresh[, "Value"],
  `Std. Error` = coef_table_no_thresh[, "Std. Error"],
  `t value` = coef_table_no_thresh[, "t value"],
  `p value` = pnorm(abs(coef_table_no_thresh[, "t value"]), lower.tail = FALSE) * 2,
  `Odds Ratio` = odds_ratios
)

# Step 5: Display
kable(coef_table_final1, digits = 3)

#
pR2(prov_model1)
null_model <- polr(provision ~ 1, data = model_data, Hess = TRUE)
anova(null_model, prov_model2)


# Residual deviance and degrees of freedom
deviance(prov_model2)
df.residual(prov_model2)

AIC(prov_model1, prov_model2)
```

```{r willingness to share MINUS gender & neighbourhood}
# Fit the logistic regression model
prov_model3 <- polr(
  provision ~ 
    blue_space_mean_frequency +
    blue_space_num_visited +
    total_activities +
    blue_space_condition +
    age +  
    blue_space_attitude +
    connection_group,
  data = model_data,
  Hess = TRUE
)

# Model summary
summary(prov_model3)
brant(prov_model3)


# Calculate p-values
coef_table <- coef(summary(prov_model3))
p_values <- pnorm(abs(coef_table[, "t value"]), lower.tail = FALSE) * 2

# Exponentiate coefficients to get odds ratios
odds_ratios <- exp(coef(prov_model3))

# Step 1: Coefficient table with p-values
coef_table <- coef(summary(prov_model3))
p_values <- pnorm(abs(coef_table[, "t value"]), lower.tail = FALSE) * 2

# Step 2: Remove threshold/intercept rows
coef_table_no_thresh <- coef_table[!grepl("\\|", rownames(coef_table)), ]

# Step 3: Calculate odds ratios only for predictors
odds_ratios <- exp(coef_table_no_thresh[, "Value"])

# Step 4: Combine into a clean table
coef_table_final1 <- cbind(
  Estimate = coef_table_no_thresh[, "Value"],
  `Std. Error` = coef_table_no_thresh[, "Std. Error"],
  `t value` = coef_table_no_thresh[, "t value"],
  `p value` = pnorm(abs(coef_table_no_thresh[, "t value"]), lower.tail = FALSE) * 2,
  `Odds Ratio` = odds_ratios
)

# Step 5: Display
kable(coef_table_final1, digits = 3)

#
pR2(prov_model3)
null_model <- polr(provision ~ 1, data = model_data, Hess = TRUE)
anova(null_model, prov_model3)


# Residual deviance and degrees of freedom
deviance(prov_model3)
df.residual(prov_model3)

AIC(prov_model1, prov_model2, prov_model3)
```

```{r willingness to share MINUS gender & neighbourhood & age}
# Fit the logistic regression model
prov_model4 <- polr(
  provision ~ 
    blue_space_mean_frequency +
    blue_space_num_visited +
    total_activities +
    blue_space_condition +
    blue_space_attitude +
    connection_group,
  data = model_data,
  Hess = TRUE
)

# Model summary
summary(prov_model4)
brant(prov_model4)

# Exponentiate coefficients to get odds ratios
odds_ratios <- exp(coef(prov_model4))

# Step 1: Coefficient table with p-values
coef_table <- coef(summary(prov_model4))
p_values <- pnorm(abs(coef_table[, "t value"]), lower.tail = FALSE) * 2

# Step 2: Remove threshold/intercept rows
coef_table_no_thresh <- coef_table[!grepl("\\|", rownames(coef_table)), ]

# Step 3: Calculate odds ratios only for predictors
odds_ratios <- exp(coef_table_no_thresh[, "Value"])

# Step 4: Combine into a clean table
coef_table_final1 <- cbind(
  Estimate = coef_table_no_thresh[, "Value"],
  `Std. Error` = coef_table_no_thresh[, "Std. Error"],
  `t value` = coef_table_no_thresh[, "t value"],
  `p value` = pnorm(abs(coef_table_no_thresh[, "t value"]), lower.tail = FALSE) * 2,
  `Odds Ratio` = odds_ratios
)

# Step 5: Display
kable(coef_table_final1, digits = 3)

#
pR2(prov_model3)
null_model <- polr(provision ~ 1, data = model_data, Hess = TRUE)
anova(null_model, prov_model4)


# Residual deviance and degrees of freedom
deviance(prov_model4)
df.residual(prov_model4)

AIC(prov_model1, prov_model2, prov_model3, prov_model4)
#
```

```{r forest plot willingness 4}
# 
coef_table <- coef(summary(prov_model4))
coef_table

# Calculate p-values from t-values
p_values <- pnorm(abs(coef_table[, "t value"]), lower.tail = FALSE) * 2

#  Add p-values to the coef_table
coef_table <- cbind(coef_table, "p value" = p_values)

# Create a data frame with CIs and significance flag
coef_df <- coef_table %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  rownames_to_column(var = "Predictor") %>%
  mutate(
    lower = Value - 1.96 * `Std. Error`,
    upper = Value + 1.96 * `Std. Error`,
    signif = ifelse(`p value` < 0.05, "Significant", "Not significant")
  )

#
coef_df <- coef_df %>%
  filter(!Predictor %in% c("Maybe_No|Provisional_Yes", "Provisional_Yes|Yes",
"blue_space_condition.Q", 
"blue_space_condition.C",
"blue_space_condition^4",
"blue_space_condition^5",
"blue_space_condition^6", 
"connection_group.Q"))

# Define your levels explicitly (including all predictors)
all_levels <- c(
  "Blue space visit mean frequency",
  "Number of different blue spaces visited",
  "Number of blue space activities",
  "Perceived UK blue spaces to be in poor condition",
  "Nature connection and engagement",
  "Willingness to provide a blue space",
  "Positive blue space attitude"
)

# Map raw predictor names to clean labels
rename_map <- c(
  "blue_space_condition" = "Perceived UK blue spaces to be in poor condition",
  "blue_space_mean_frequency" = "Blue space visit mean frequency",
  "blue_space_num_visited" = "Number of different blue spaces visited",
  "total_activities" = "Number of blue space activities",
  "provision.L" = "Willingness to provide a blue space",
  "blue_space_attitude" = "Positive blue space attitude",
  "connection_group.L" = "Nature connection and engagement"
)

# Add clean labels and set factor levels
coef_df <- coef_df %>%
  mutate(
    Predictor_clean = rename_map[Predictor],
    Predictor_clean = ifelse(is.na(Predictor_clean), Predictor, Predictor_clean),
    Predictor_clean = factor(Predictor_clean, levels = all_levels)
  )

# Add a blank row if "Willingness to provide a blue space" is missing
if (!"Willingness to provide a blue space" %in% coef_df$Predictor_clean) {
  coef_df <- bind_rows(coef_df, tibble(
    Predictor_clean = factor("Willingness to provide a blue space", levels = all_levels),
    Value = NA_real_,
    lower = NA_real_,
    upper = NA_real_,
    signif = NA_character_
  ))
}
p2 <- ggplot(coef_df, aes(x = factor(Predictor_clean, levels = c(
  "Blue space visit mean frequency",
  "Number of different blue spaces visited",
  "Number of blue space activities",
  "Perceived UK blue spaces to be in poor condition",
  "Nature connection and engagement",
  "Willingness to provide a blue space",
  "Positive blue space attitude"
)), y = Value, color = signif)
) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper), width = 0.2) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Provide Blue Space",
    x = "Predictor",
    y = "",
    color = "Significance"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Significant" = "#0072B2", "Not significant" = "grey60"))
p2

## Participants who believe blue spaces are in poorer condition tend to report lower nature connection/Participants who view blue spaces as being in good condition tend to feel more connected to nature.
```

## Blue space attitude model 

```{r attitude model FULL}
# Variance Inflation Factor to check multicollinearity
lm_model_vif <- lm(
  rep(1, nrow(model_data)) ~ 
    blue_space_attitude +
    heard_rewilding +
    gender +
    neighbourhood +
    blue_space_mean_frequency +
    blue_space_num_visited +
    total_activities +
    blue_space_condition +
    provision +
    connection_group +
    age,
  data = model_data
)
print(vif(lm_model_vif))

# reflect 
model_data$blue_space_attitude_reflect <- max(model_data$blue_space_attitude, na.rm = TRUE) + 1 - model_data$blue_space_attitude
hist(model_data$blue_space_attitude_reflect)

# Fit Gamma GLM
glm_model_gamma <- glm(blue_space_attitude_reflect ~ 
                        blue_space_mean_frequency +
                        blue_space_num_visited +
                        total_activities +
                        gender + 
                        blue_space_condition +
                        neighbourhood + 
                        age +
                        provision +
                        connection_group,
                        family = Gamma(link = "log"), data = model_data)
 
# Fit Inverse Gaussian GLM
glm_model_inv_gauss <- glm(blue_space_attitude_reflect ~ 
                            blue_space_mean_frequency +
                            blue_space_num_visited +
                            total_activities +
                            gender + 
                            blue_space_condition +
                            neighbourhood + 
                            age +
                            provision +
                            blue_space_condition,
                            family = inverse.gaussian(link = "log"), data = model_data)

# Summaries
summary(glm_model_gamma)
summary(glm_model_inv_gauss)

# Model comparison using AIC
aic_gamma <- AIC(glm_model_gamma)
aic_inv_gauss <- AIC(glm_model_inv_gauss)
print(paste("AIC Gamma model:", round(aic_gamma, 2)))
print(paste("AIC Inverse Gaussian model:", round(aic_inv_gauss, 2)))

# Plot diagnostics for inverse Gaussian model
plot(glm_model_inv_gauss)

#
null_model <- glm(blue_space_attitude_reflect ~ 1, 
                  family = inverse.gaussian(link = "log"), 
                  data = model_data)
anova(null_model, glm_model_inv_gauss, test = "Chisq")

# Pseudo R² for inverse Gaussian model
null_dev <- glm_model_inv_gauss$null.deviance
res_dev <- glm_model_inv_gauss$deviance
pseudo_r2 <- 1 - (res_dev / null_dev)
print(paste("Pseudo R-squared:", round(pseudo_r2, 4)))

# Tidy the model output
model_tidy <- tidy(glm_model_inv_gauss)

#
kable(model_tidy, digits = 3, caption = "Inverse Gaussian GLM Summary")

#
sim_res <- simulateResiduals(glm_model_inv_gauss)
plot(sim_res)
testUniformity(sim_res)   # Should return non-significant
testDispersion(sim_res)   # Non-significant = good
testOutliers(sim_res)     # Checks for unusual points

```

```{r attitude model MINUS Gender}
# Fit Inverse Gaussian GLM
glm_model_inv_gauss1 <- glm(blue_space_attitude_reflect ~ 
                            blue_space_mean_frequency +
                            blue_space_num_visited +
                            total_activities +
                            blue_space_condition +
                            neighbourhood + 
                            age +
                            provision +
                            connection_group,
                            family = inverse.gaussian(link = "log"), data = model_data)

# Summary
summary(glm_model_inv_gauss1)
AIC(glm_model_inv_gauss1, glm_model_inv_gauss)

# Plot diagnostics for inverse Gaussian model
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
plot(glm_model_inv_gauss1)

#
null_model <- glm(blue_space_attitude_reflect ~ 1, 
                  family = inverse.gaussian(link = "log"), 
                  data = model_data)
anova(null_model, glm_model_inv_gauss1, test = "Chisq")

# Pseudo R² for inverse Gaussian model
null_dev <- glm_model_inv_gauss1$null.deviance
res_dev <- glm_model_inv_gauss1$deviance
pseudo_r2 <- 1 - (res_dev / null_dev)
print(paste("Pseudo R-squared:", round(pseudo_r2, 4)))

#
sim_res <- simulateResiduals(glm_model_inv_gauss1)
plot(sim_res)
testUniformity(sim_res)   # Should return non-significant
testDispersion(sim_res)   # Non-significant = good
testOutliers(sim_res)     # Checks for unusual points


```

```{r attitude model MINUS Gender & neighbourhood}
# Fit Inverse Gaussian GLM
glm_model_inv_gauss2 <- glm(blue_space_attitude_reflect ~ 
                            blue_space_total_frequency +
                            blue_space_num_visited +
                            total_activities +
                            blue_space_condition +
                            age +
                            provision +
                            connection_group,
                            family = inverse.gaussian(link = "log"), data = model_data)

# Summaries
summary(glm_model_inv_gauss2)
AIC(glm_model_inv_gauss1, glm_model_inv_gauss2, glm_model_inv_gauss)

# Plot diagnostics for inverse Gaussian model
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
plot(glm_model_inv_gauss2)

#
null_model <- glm(blue_space_attitude_reflect ~ 1, 
                  family = inverse.gaussian(link = "log"), 
                  data = model_data)
anova(null_model, glm_model_inv_gauss2, test = "Chisq")

# Pseudo R² for inverse Gaussian model
null_dev <- glm_model_inv_gauss2$null.deviance
res_dev <- glm_model_inv_gauss2$deviance
pseudo_r2 <- 1 - (res_dev / null_dev)
print(paste("Pseudo R-squared:", round(pseudo_r2, 4)))

#
sim_res <- simulateResiduals(glm_model_inv_gauss2)
plot(sim_res)
testUniformity(sim_res)   # Should return non-significant
testDispersion(sim_res)   # Non-significant = good
testOutliers(sim_res)     # Checks for unusual points

```

```{r attitude model MINUS Gender & neighbourhood & age}
# Fit Inverse Gaussian GLM (over dispersed)
glm_model_inv_gauss3 <- glm(blue_space_attitude_reflect ~ 
                            blue_space_mean_frequency +
                            blue_space_num_visited +
                            total_activities +
                            blue_space_condition +
                            provision +
                            connection_group,
                            family = inverse.gaussian(link = "log"), data = model_data)


# Summary
summary(glm_model_inv_gauss3)

AIC(glm_model_inv_gauss, glm_model_inv_gauss1, glm_model_inv_gauss2, glm_model_inv_gauss3)

# Plot diagnostics for inverse Gaussian model
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
plot(glm_model_inv_gauss3)

#
null_model <- glm(blue_space_attitude_reflect ~ 1, 
                  family = inverse.gaussian(link = "log"), 
                  data = model_data)
anova(null_model, glm_model_inv_gauss3, test = "Chisq")

# Pseudo R² for inverse Gaussian model
null_dev <- glm_model_inv_gauss3$null.deviance
res_dev <- glm_model_inv_gauss3$deviance
pseudo_r2 <- 1 - (res_dev / null_dev)
print(paste("Pseudo R-squared:", round(pseudo_r2, 4)))

#
sim_res <- simulateResiduals(glm_model_inv_gauss3)
plot(sim_res)
testUniformity(sim_res)   # Should return non-significant
testDispersion(sim_res)   # Non-significant = good
testOutliers(sim_res)     # Checks for unusual points
# slightly under dispersed but otherwise fineggsave("dharma_plot.png", sim_res = dh, width = 16, height = 8, dpi = 400)
```

```{r attitude plot}
# Summary
summary(glm_model_inv_gauss3)

# Define full set of labels (including the missing one)
term_labels <- c(
  "blue_space_mean_frequency" = "Blue space visit mean frequency",
  "blue_space_num_visited" = "Number of different blue spaces visited",
  "total_activities" = "Number of blue space activities",
  "blue_space_condition" = "Perceived UK blue spaces to be in poor condition",
  "connection_group.L" = "Nature connection and engagement",
  "provision.L" = "Willingness to provide a blue space",
  "blue_space_attitude" = "Positive blue space attitude"  # <-- added
)

# Full desired order
all_levels <- c(
  "Blue space visit mean frequency",
  "Number of different blue spaces visited",
  "Number of blue space activities",
  "Perceived UK blue spaces to be in poor condition",
  "Nature connection and engagement",
  "Willingness to provide a blue space",
  "Positive blue space attitude"
)

# Process model output
inv_gauss_tidy <- tidy(glm_model_inv_gauss3) %>%
  filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%
  filter(term %in% names(term_labels)) %>%
  mutate(
    estimate = -estimate,
    sig = ifelse(p.value < 0.05, "p < 0.05", "n.s."),
    term_clean = term_labels[term]
  )

# Add blank row if missing
if (!"Positive blue space attitude" %in% inv_gauss_tidy$term_clean) {
  inv_gauss_tidy <- bind_rows(inv_gauss_tidy, tibble(
    term_clean = "Positive blue space attitude",
    estimate = NA_real_,
    std.error = NA_real_,
    sig = NA_character_
  ))
}

# Ensure correct ordering
inv_gauss_tidy <- inv_gauss_tidy %>%
  mutate(term_clean = factor(term_clean, levels = all_levels))
inv_gauss_tidy
# Plot
p3 <- ggplot(inv_gauss_tidy, aes(x = term_clean, y = estimate, color = sig)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = estimate - std.error, ymax = estimate + std.error), width = 0.2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("p < 0.05" = "#0072B2", "n.s." = "grey60"), na.value = "grey90") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Positive attitude towards blue space",
    x = NULL,
    y = "",
    color = "Significance"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14)

print(p3)

```

## Final model plot

```{r final plot}
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)

# 
base_clean_theme <- theme_bw() +
  theme(
    plot.title   = element_text(hjust = 0.5, colour = "black", face = "bold", size = 14),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 16, colour = "black"),
    axis.text.x  = element_text(size = 14, colour = "black"),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

# 
p1_clean <- p1 +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
  labs(title = "Nature connection and engagement") +
  base_clean_theme +
  theme(
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y  = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y = element_blank()
  )

p2_clean <- p2 +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
  labs(title = "Willingness to provide blue space") +
  base_clean_theme +
  theme(
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y  = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y = element_blank()
  )

p3_clean <- p3 +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
  labs(title = "Positive blue space attitude") +
  base_clean_theme +
  theme(
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 16, colour = "black"),
    axis.text.y  = element_text(size = 14, colour = "black")
  )

# 
combined_plot <- p3_clean + p1_clean + p2_clean +
  plot_layout(ncol = 3)

combined_plot

#
ggsave("combined_plot.png", plot = combined_plot, width = 16, height = 8, dpi = 400)
```

## Attitudes plot

```{r diverging bar plot blue attitude}
attitude_plot <- read_csv("attitude_plot.csv")
View(attitude_plot)

# Pivot to long format
# Specify exact columns instead of starts_with
long_data <- attitude_plot %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(
      "12.1. Cities should have more blue spaces",
      "12.2. Blue spaces are important for nature",
      "12.3. Blue spaces in the UK are in poor condition",
      "12.4. Blue spaces are important for my health and wellbeing",
      "12.5. We should “rewild” UK blue spaces"
    ),
    names_to = "item",
    values_to = "response"
  )

# Filter out NA and non-1:7 values
long_data <- long_data %>%
  filter(!is.na(response), response %in% 1:7)

# Convert to factor
long_data$response <- factor(long_data$response, levels = 1:7)

# Create a named vector
item_labels <- c(
  "12.1. Cities should have more blue spaces" = "Cities should have more blue spaces",
  "12.2. Blue spaces are important for nature" = "Blue spaces are important for nature",
  "12.3. Blue spaces in the UK are in poor condition" = "Blue spaces in the UK are in poor condition",
  "12.4. Blue spaces are important for my health and wellbeing" = "Blue spaces are important for my health and wellbeing",
  "12.5. We should “rewild” UK blue spaces" = "We should “rewild” UK blue spaces"
)

# Use dplyr::mutate + base R subsetting
long_data <- long_data %>%
  mutate(item = item_labels[item])

# Check total number of responses per item (should match participant count)
long_data %>%
  group_by(item) %>%
  summarise(n_responses = n()) %>%
  print()

# Convert response to numeric for calculations
long_data <- long_data %>%
  mutate(response_num = as.numeric(as.character(response)))

# Summary stats for each item
likert_summary <- long_data %>%
  group_by(item) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean = round(mean(response_num, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    sd = round(sd(response_num, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Print the summary
print(likert_summary)

# Calculate proportions
prop_data <- long_data %>%
  group_by(item, response) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(item) %>%
  mutate(prop = n / sum(n))

# Create diverging categories
prop_data <- prop_data %>%
  mutate(response_cat = case_when(
    response %in% c("1", "2", "3") ~ "Disagree",
    response == "4"               ~ "Neutral",
    response %in% c("5", "6", "7") ~ "Agree"
  ))

# Optional: set diverging bar colors
likert_colors <- c(
  "1" = "#9466a1",  # lighter purple (was #762a83)
  "2" = "#a984bd",  # lighter lavender (was #9970ab)
  "3" = "#d8bfd8",  # pale purple (same as before)
  "4" = "#f0f0f0",  # light grey
  "5" = "#bdbdbd",  # medium grey
  "6" = "#9ec9c9",  # your teal
  "7" = "#7ea1a1"   # your dark teal
)

# Plot
p1 <- ggplot(prop_data, aes(x = prop, y = fct_rev(item), fill = response)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = likert_colors,
    name = "Response",
    labels = c(
      "1 Strongly Disagree", "2", "3", "4 Neutral", 
      "5", "6", "7 Strongly Agree"
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Proportion of responses", 
    y = NULL,
    title = "",
    subtitle = "",
    caption = "1 = Strongly Disagree, 7 = Strongly Agree"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 14) +  # base text size increase
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",      # move legend to right
    legend.direction = "vertical",  # vertical layout
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 14),  # bigger x-axis labels
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 14),  # bigger y-axis labels
    legend.text = element_text(size = 12),  # bigger legend text
    legend.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold")
  )

p1
#
ggsave("attitude_plot.png", plot = p1, width = 15, height = 8, dpi = 500)
```

```{r boxplot attitude}
attitude_plot <- read_csv("attitude_plot.csv")
View(attitude_plot)


# Pivot to long format
# Specify exact columns instead of starts_with
long_data <- attitude_plot %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(
      "12.1. Cities should have more blue spaces",
      "12.2. Blue spaces are important for nature",
      "12.3. Blue spaces in the UK are in poor condition",
      "12.4. Blue spaces are important for my health and wellbeing",
      "12.5. We should “rewild” UK blue spaces"
    ),
    names_to = "item",
    values_to = "response"
  )

library(ggplot2)

# Define colours for your items (add more if you have >3)
my_cols <- c(
  "12.1. Cities should have more blue spaces"                   = "#9ec9c9",
  "12.2. Blue spaces are important for nature"                  = "#7ea1a1",
  "12.3. Blue spaces in the UK are in poor condition"            = "#797d7d",
  "12.4. Blue spaces are important for my health and wellbeing"  = "#b3b3b3",
  "12.5. We should “rewild” UK blue spaces"                      = "#5f6f6f"
)

ggplot(long_data, aes(x = item, y = response, fill = item)) +
  geom_half_violin(side = "r", trim = FALSE, adjust = 0.8, color = NA, alpha = 0.7) +  # right half violin
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = my_cols) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 8), breaks = 0:8) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Disagree ← → Agree",
    fill = "Question",
    title = ""
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 14),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold")
  )
```

```{r bar and box plot}
# Let's summarise counts & percentages for each item × response
plot_data <- long_data %>%
  group_by(item, response) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop_last") %>%
  mutate(
    pct = n / sum(n) * 100
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# Bar plot of responses per item, with % labels
p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = factor(response), y = pct, fill = item)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = sprintf("%.1f%%", pct)),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.9),
    vjust = -0.2,
    size = 3.5
  ) +
  # Overlay boxplot of raw response distributions
  geom_boxplot(
    data = long_data,
    aes(x = factor(response), y = as.numeric(response), group = item),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    width = 0.3,
    alpha = 0.5
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ item, ncol = 1, scales = "free_x") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = my_cols) +
  labs(
    x = "Response",
    y = "Percentage of respondents",
    title = "Distribution of Attitudes to Blue Spaces",
    fill = "Question"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12)
  )

p
```

```{r final blue space distribution}
means <- long_data %>%
  group_by(item) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_resp = mean(as.numeric(response), na.rm = TRUE),
    se = sd(as.numeric(response), na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    ci_lower = mean_resp - 1.96 * se,
    ci_upper = mean_resp + 1.96 * se,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = factor(response), y = pct, fill = item)) +
  # Bars of % responses
  geom_col(alpha = 0.7, show.legend = FALSE) +
  # % labels on bars
  geom_text(
    aes(label = sprintf("%.1f%%", pct)),
    vjust = -0.2,
    size = 3.5
  ) +
  # Overlay mean + 95% CI as horizontal point + error bar
  geom_errorbarh(
    data = means,
    aes(
      x = mean_resp,
      xmin = ci_lower,      # Fixed: use ci_lower directly
      xmax = ci_upper,      # Fixed: use ci_upper directly
      y = max(plot_data$pct) + 5
    ),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    height = 8,             # Made taller for visibility
    colour = "darkblue",
    size = 0.8              # Made thicker for visibility
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = means,
    aes(x = mean_resp, y = max(plot_data$pct) + 5),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    colour = "darkblue",
    size = 2
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ item, ncol = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = my_cols) +
  scale_x_discrete(limits = as.character(1:7)) +
  labs(
    x = "Disagree ↔ Agree",
    y = "Percentage of respondents",
    title = "Distribution of Attitudes to Blue Spaces"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
    panel.border = element_rect(colour = "black", fill = NA, size = 0.5),
    panel.grid = element_blank()
  )
```

## Visit frequency

```{r diverging bar visit frequency}
long_visits <- model_data %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(
      "10.1. Rivers, canals and dykes",
      "10.2. Park lakes",
      "10.3. Coastal areas",
      "10.4. Estuaries",
      "10.5. Marinas"
    ),
    names_to = "type",
    values_to = "frequency"
  ) %>%
  mutate(frequency = case_when(
    frequency == 52 ~ "Weekly",
    frequency == 12 ~ "Monthly",
    frequency == 2  ~ "Every 6 months",
    frequency == 1  ~ "Once a year",
    frequency == 0  ~ "Never",
    TRUE ~ as.character(frequency)
  )) %>%
  mutate(
    frequency = factor(frequency, levels = c("Weekly", "Monthly", "Every 6 months", "Once a year", "Never")),
    type = factor(type)
  )

# Define your custom colors here (adjust hex codes or names)
custom_colors <- c(
  "10.1. Rivers, canals and dykes" = "#91bfdb",  # blue
  "10.2. Park lakes" = "#33a02caa",                 # green
  "10.3. Coastal areas" = "#4575b4",               # red
  "10.4. Estuaries" = "#fc8d59",                   # orange
  "10.5. Marinas" = "grey"                      # purple
)

ggplot(long_visits, aes(x = frequency, fill = type)) +
  geom_bar(position = position_dodge(width = 0.7), width = 0.6) +  # dodge width < 1 adds gaps
  scale_fill_manual(values = custom_colors) +
  labs(
    x = "Visiting Frequency",
    y = "Number of Respondents",
    fill = "Blue Space Type",
    title = "Visits to Blue Spaces by Frequency and Type"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

# Add numeric score for each frequency
long_visits_summary <- long_visits %>%
  mutate(freq_score = case_when(
    frequency == "Never" ~ 0,
    frequency == "Once a year" ~ 1,
    frequency == "Every 6 months" ~ 2,
    frequency == "Monthly" ~ 12,
    frequency == "Weekly" ~ 52
  )) %>%
  group_by(type) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_score = mean(freq_score, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = n()
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(mean_score))
print(long_visits_summary)

# Create summary table manually or from your data
visit_summary <- tibble::tibble(
  type = c("Rivers, canals and dykes", "Coastal areas", "Park lakes", "Estuaries", "Marinas"),
  mean_annual_visits = c(24.28, 15.35, 15.01, 13.63, 10.85)
)

ggplot(visit_summary, aes(x = reorder(type, mean_annual_visits), y = mean_annual_visits)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#9ec9c9") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Mean Annual Visits",
    title = ""
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 14),    # X-axis title
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14),    # Y-axis title
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 14),     # X-axis tick labels
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 14),     # Y-axis tick labels
    legend.title = element_text(size = 14),    # Legend title
    legend.text = element_text(size = 14)      # Legend items
  )

```

```{r stepwise table}
model_list <- list(
  sig_model1 = sig_model1,
  sig_model2 = sig_model2,
  sig_model3 = sig_model3,
  sig_model4 = sig_model4,
  prov_model1 = prov_model1,
  prov_model2 = prov_model2,
  prov_model3 = prov_model3,
  prov_model4 = prov_model4,
  glm_model_inv_gauss  = glm_model_inv_gauss,
  glm_model_inv_gauss1 = glm_model_inv_gauss1,
  glm_model_inv_gauss2 = glm_model_inv_gauss2,
  glm_model_inv_gauss3 = glm_model_inv_gauss3
)

library(dplyr)
library(purrr)

model_comp <- tibble(
  model   = names(model_list),
  AIC     = map_dbl(model_list, AIC),
  logLik  = map_dbl(model_list, ~ as.numeric(logLik(.x))),
  k       = map_dbl(model_list, ~ attr(logLik(.x), "df")),
  formula = map_chr(model_list, ~ paste(deparse(formula(.x)), collapse = " "))
) %>%
  arrange(AIC)
```
